verified_citation: Naidech AM, Jovanovic B, Wartenberg KE, et al. Higher hemoglobin is associated with improved outcome after subarachnoid hemorrhage. Crit Care Med. 2007;35(10):2383-2389.
country_setting: Single neurological ICU, Columbia University, USA
design: Prospective registry with automated laboratory retrieval
time_zero_windows: Hospital course; outcomes at discharge/14 days and 3 months
exposure_or_intervention: Mean and nadir hemoglobin; transfusion context
outcomes_horizon: Modified Rankin Scale and mortality through 3 months
statistical_methods: Adjusted regression including Hunt-Hess grade, age, diabetes, and cerebral infarction
main_estimate_uncertainty: Higher hemoglobin associated with lower adjusted risk of poor outcome; abstract gives group means and p<0.001 but not adjusted effect CI
limitations_risk_of_bias: Observational association, time-varying treatment/confounding, single center; cannot infer transfusion benefit
